annotations.label: Adjunction between categories

anchor.snippet: is a pair of functors (assumed to be covariant )

annotations.note: `Adjunction F G` (notation `F ⊣ G`) bundles an adjunction between functors `F : C ⥤ D` and `G : D ⥤ C`.

annotations.label: Left and right adjoint

anchor.snippet: is called a left adjoint functor or left adjoint to

annotations.note: `Functor.IsLeftAdjoint`/`Functor.IsRightAdjoint` assert that a functor is a left/right adjoint.

annotations.label: Equivalence gives an adjunction

anchor.snippet: every equivalence gives an adjunction

annotations.note: `Equivalence.toAdjunction` produces `e.functor ⊣ e.inverse` from any equivalence.

annotations.label: Right adjunct (Mac Lane)

anchor.snippet: the right adjunct of

annotations.note: `Adjunction.homEquiv` is the natural bijection `(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)` sending a morphism to its (right) adjunct.

annotations.label: Adjoining identity to a rng

anchor.snippet: Then F is the left adjoint functor of

annotations.note: The unitization construction and its universal property exist, but there is no category of rngs with a formalized adjunction to unital rings.

annotations.label: Adjoint functors occur in pairs

anchor.snippet: adjoint functors occur in pairs

annotations.note: An `Adjunction` `F ⊣ G` is precisely such a pair, and `Adjunction.isLeftAdjoint`/`isRightAdjoint` record that each member determines the other's role.

annotations.label: Left adjoint via universal morphisms

anchor.snippet: is a left adjoint functor if for each object

annotations.note: Initial objects of all `StructuredArrow` categories on `G` yield a left adjoint via `leftAdjointOfStructuredArrowInitials`.

annotations.label: Right adjoint via universal morphisms

anchor.snippet: is a right adjoint functor if for each object

annotations.note: Terminal objects of all `CostructuredArrow` categories yield a right adjoint via `rightAdjointOfCostructuredArrowTerminals`.

annotations.label: Left adjoint iff right adjoint

anchor.snippet: as the terminology implies

annotations.note: `Adjunction.isLeftAdjoint` and `isRightAdjoint` show `F ⊣ G` makes `F` a left adjoint and `G` a right adjoint simultaneously.

annotations.label: Adjunction via hom-sets

anchor.snippet: can be defined as consisting of two functors

annotations.note: `CoreHomEquiv` + `Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv` build an adjunction from a natural family of hom-set bijections.

anchors.snippet: can be defined as consisting of two functors

annotations.label: Adjunction via counit–unit

anchor.snippet: A third way of defining an adjunction between two categories

annotations.note: Mathlib's primitive `Adjunction` structure is exactly the counit–unit definition (fields `unit`, `counit`, triangle identities).

anchors.snippet: A third way of defining an adjunction between two categories

annotations.label: Triangle identities

anchor.snippet: They are sometimes called the triangle identities

annotations.note: `Adjunction.left_triangle`/`right_triangle` (and the `*_triangle_components` structure fields) are the two triangle identities.

annotations.label: Tensor-hom adjunction

anchor.snippet: in the category of abelian groups

annotations.note: `ihom.adjunction` gives `(A ⊗ -) ⊣ ihom A` in any monoidal closed category; abelian groups (ℤ-modules) are the special case via the `ModuleCat` monoidal closed instance.

annotations.label: Free group functor left adjoint to forgetful

anchor.snippet: be the functor assigning to each set Y the free group

annotations.note: `GrpCat.adj : free ⊣ forget GrpCat` is the free-group/forgetful adjunction.

annotations.label: Free objects are left adjoints

anchor.snippet: Free objects are all examples of a left adjoint to a forgetful functor

annotations.note: Many specific free⊣forgetful adjunctions exist (groups, monoids, modules, Eilenberg–Moore `Monad.adj`), but there is no single general theorem asserting all free constructions are left adjoints.

annotations.label: Limit functor right adjoint to diagonal

anchor.snippet: Any limit functor is right adjoint to a corresponding diagonal functor

annotations.note: `constLimAdj : const J ⊣ lim` exhibits the limit functor as right adjoint to the (diagonal) constant functor.

annotations.label: Products

anchor.snippet: the product group X 1 × X 2

annotations.note: Categorical products exist (`HasProducts`); the diagonal–product adjunction is the discrete-shape special case of `constLimAdj`, not stated separately for binary products.

annotations.label: Kernels

anchor.snippet: Consider the category D of homomorphisms of abelian groups.

annotations.note: Kernels exist as limits, but the framing of the kernel functor as an adjoint over the category of homomorphisms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Colimit functor left adjoint to diagonal

anchor.snippet: Any colimit functor is left adjoint to a corresponding diagonal functor

annotations.note: `colimConstAdj : colim ⊣ const J` exhibits the colimit functor as left adjoint to the diagonal.

annotations.label: Coproducts

anchor.snippet: of abelian groups their direct sum

annotations.note: Coproducts exist (`HasCoproducts`); the diagonal–coproduct adjunction is the discrete-shape special case of `colimConstAdj`.

annotations.label: Adjoining identity to a rng

anchor.snippet: This constructs a left adjoint to the functor taking a ring to the underlying rng.

annotations.note: `Unitization` realizes the construction with its universal property, but no category-of-rngs adjunction is formalized.

annotations.label: Adjoining identity to a semigroup

anchor.snippet: This construction gives a functor that is a left adjoint to the functor taking a monoid to the underlying semigroup.

annotations.note: `MonCat.adjoinOneAdj : adjoinOne ⊣ forget₂ MonCat Semigrp` is exactly this adjunction.

annotations.label: Ring extensions

anchor.snippet: Then F is left adjoint to the forgetful functor

annotations.note: For modules over commutative rings, `extendRestrictScalarsAdj` shows extension of scalars `S ⊗_R -` is left adjoint to restriction of scalars.

annotations.label: Tensor products

anchor.snippet: is a right adjoint to F

annotations.note: `restrictCoextendScalarsAdj` gives the right-adjoint (coextension/Hom) counterpart of the change-of-rings adjunctions for modules.

annotations.label: Monoid and group rings

anchor.snippet: The integral monoid ring construction gives a functor from monoids to rings.

annotations.note: The monoid-algebra universal property is formalized (e.g. `MonoidAlgebra.liftMagma`/`lift`), but not packaged as a categorical adjunction between monoids and rings.

annotations.label: Field of fractions

anchor.snippet: from fields has a left adjoint

annotations.note: `IsFractionRing`/`FractionRing` provide the field-of-fractions universal property, but no categorical adjunction with the category of integral domains is formalized.

annotations.label: Polynomial rings

anchor.snippet: it assigns to every ring R the pair (R[x],x)

annotations.note: The universal property of `R[x]` (evaluation) is formalized, but the adjunction with a category of pointed rings is not.

annotations.label: Abelianization

anchor.snippet: It has a left adjoint called abelianization

annotations.note: `GrpCat.abelianizeAdj : abelianize ⊣ forget₂ CommGrpCat GrpCat` is the abelianization adjunction.

annotations.label: Grothendieck group

anchor.snippet: takes the underlying monoid (ignoring inverses) has a left adjoint

annotations.note: The Grothendieck group construction and universal property exist, but group completion as a left adjoint to the forgetful functor is not formalized as an adjunction.

annotations.label: Frobenius reciprocity

anchor.snippet: Frobenius reciprocity in the representation theory of groups

annotations.note: `Rep.indResAdjunction : indFunctor ⊣ resFunctor` is precisely the induction–restriction (Frobenius reciprocity) adjunction.

annotations.label: Forgetful functor with left and right adjoint

anchor.snippet: creating the discrete space on a set Y , and a right adjoint H

annotations.note: `TopCat.adj₁ : discrete ⊣ forget` and `TopCat.adj₂ : forget ⊣ trivial` give the discrete left adjoint and indiscrete right adjoint to the forgetful functor.

annotations.label: Suspensions and loop spaces

anchor.snippet: The suspension functor is therefore left adjoint to the loop space functor

annotations.note: The suspension–loop space adjunction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stone–Čech compactification

anchor.snippet: the inclusion functor to the category of topological spaces

annotations.note: The Stone–Čech universal property (`stoneCechUnit`, `stoneCechExtend`) is formalized, but not as a categorical adjunction with the inclusion functor.

annotations.label: Direct and inverse images of sheaves

anchor.snippet: f −1 is left adjoint to f ∗

annotations.note: `TopCat.Sheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback A f ⊣ pushforward A f` is the inverse-image/direct-image sheaf adjunction.

annotations.label: Soberification

anchor.snippet: that is known as soberification

annotations.note: Sober spaces are defined (`Topology.Sober`), but the soberification reflector/adjunction is not formalized.

annotations.label: Galois connection

anchor.snippet: is called a Galois connection

annotations.note: `GaloisConnection l u` is the order-theoretic adjunction `l a ≤ b ↔ a ≤ u b`.

annotations.label: Galois connection gives closure operators

anchor.snippet: Any Galois connection gives rise to closure operators

annotations.note: `GaloisConnection.closureOperator` builds the induced closure operator `u ∘ l`.

annotations.label: Syntax and semantics are adjoint

anchor.snippet: syntax and semantics are adjoint

annotations.note: The syntax–semantics Galois connection of formal logic is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence yields adjoint pair

anchor.snippet: the two functors F and G form an adjoint pair

annotations.note: `Equivalence.toAdjunction` turns the equivalence's functor/inverse into an adjoint pair.

anchors.snippet: the two functors F and G form an adjoint pair

annotations.label: Connected components functor

anchor.snippet: which assigns to a category its set of connected components is left-adjoint

annotations.note: `ConnectedComponents` and the bijection `typeToCatHomEquiv` give the π₀ ⊣ discrete hom-set adjunction, but it is not packaged as a `Cat`-level `Adjunction`.

anchors.snippet: which assigns to a category its set of connected components is left-adjoint

annotations.label: Currying in cartesian closed category

anchor.snippet: This pair is often referred to as currying and uncurrying

annotations.note: `ihom.adjunction` with `MonoidalClosed.curry`/`uncurry` gives the currying adjunction; cartesian closed categories are the cartesian-monoidal special case (`CartesianClosed`).

anchors.snippet: This pair is often referred to as currying and uncurrying

annotations.label: Quantifiers as adjoints to pullback

anchor.snippet: quantifiers are identified with adjoints to the pullback functor

annotations.note: The poset-level existential adjoint `Set.image_preimage` exists, but the hyperdoctrine framing of ∃/∀ as adjoints to the pullback functor is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: quantifiers are identified with adjoints to the pullback functor

annotations.label: Direct image as left adjoint in Set

anchor.snippet: the category of sets and functions

annotations.note: `Set.image_preimage : GaloisConnection (image f) (preimage f)` is the direct-image-left-adjoint-to-preimage as a poset adjunction.

anchors.snippet: the category of sets and functions

annotations.label: Expectation as adjoint of Dirac delta

anchor.snippet: the Dirac delta measure defines a functor

annotations.note: The expectation/Dirac-delta adjunction from categorical probability is not formalized.

annotations.label: Full data of an adjunction

anchor.snippet: An adjunction between categories C and D consists of

annotations.note: `CoreHomEquivUnitCounit` (with `Adjunction.mk'` and `homEquiv`) bundles functors, unit, counit, and the hom-set equivalence with their compatibilities.

annotations.label: Universal morphisms induce hom-set adjunction

anchor.snippet: one may construct the induced hom-set adjunction by doing the following steps

annotations.note: `leftAdjointOfEquiv`/`adjunctionOfEquivLeft` (and `Comma.adjunctionOfStructuredArrowInitials`) construct the adjunction from universal/initial-morphism data.

annotations.label: Counit–unit induces hom-set adjunction

anchor.snippet: we can construct a hom-set adjunction by finding the natural transformation

annotations.note: `Adjunction.homEquiv` constructs the natural hom-set bijection from the unit/counit (with `homEquiv_unit`/`homEquiv_counit`).

anchors.snippet: we can construct a hom-set adjunction by finding the natural transformation

annotations.label: Hom-set adjunction induces counit–unit

anchor.section: Hom-set adjunction induces all of the above
anchor.snippet: which defines families of initial and terminal morphisms

annotations.note: `Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv` produces the unit and counit (and triangle identities) from a natural hom-set equivalence.

anchors.section: Hom-set adjunction induces all of the above
anchors.snippet: which defines families of initial and terminal morphisms

annotations.label: Freyd adjoint functor theorem

anchor.snippet: the adjoint functor theorem of Peter J. Freyd

annotations.note: This is the general adjoint functor theorem: a limit-preserving functor satisfying the solution set condition has a left adjoint.

annotations.label: Adjoints for locally presentable categories

anchor.snippet: F has a right adjoint if and only if F preserves small colimits

annotations.note: Locally presentable categories are defined, but the adjoint functor theorem characterizing adjoints via (co)limit preservation for them is not formalized.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of adjoints

anchor.snippet: then G and G ′ are naturally isomorphic

annotations.note: `rightAdjointUniq` (and `leftAdjointUniq`) give the natural isomorphism between any two right (resp. left) adjoints of a functor.

annotations.label: Adjointness preserved under natural isomorphism

anchor.snippet: then F is also left adjoint to G ′

annotations.note: `ofNatIsoRight` (and `ofNatIsoLeft`) transport an adjunction along a natural isomorphism of either functor.

anchors.snippet: then F is also left adjoint to G ′

annotations.label: Composition of adjunctions

anchor.snippet: Adjunctions can be composed in a natural fashion.

annotations.note: `Adjunction.comp` composes `F ⊣ G` and `H ⊣ I` to `F ⋙ H ⊣ I ⋙ G`.

anchors.snippet: Adjunctions can be composed in a natural fashion.

annotations.label: Adjoints preserve limits/colimits

anchor.snippet: every functor that has a left adjoint (and therefore is a right adjoint) is continuous

annotations.note: `rightAdjoint_preservesLimits` and `leftAdjoint_preservesColimits` give RAPL/LAPC.

annotations.label: Right adjoint of additive functor is additive

anchor.snippet: then G is also an additive functor

annotations.note: `Adjunction.right_adjoint_additive` shows the right adjoint of an additive functor is additive (and dually `left_adjoint_additive`).

annotations.label: Adjoints between additive categories are additive

anchor.snippet: then any pair of adjoint functors between them are automatically additive

annotations.note: The pieces exist (adjoints preserve biproducts and `additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts`), but the unconditional 'adjoints between additive categories are automatically additive' statement is not packaged as one lemma.

annotations.label: Universal morphisms yield left adjoint

anchor.snippet: then G has a left adjoint

annotations.note: `adjunctionOfStructuredArrowInitials` gives a left adjoint to `G` from initial objects in all structured-arrow categories.

annotations.label: Equivalence half is left adjoint

anchor.snippet: is one half of an equivalence of categories then it is the left adjoint

annotations.note: `Equivalence.isLeftAdjoint_functor` shows the functor of an equivalence is a left adjoint (and `isRightAdjoint_inverse` dually).

annotations.label: Adjunction restricts to equivalence

anchor.snippet: extends an equivalence of certain subcategories

annotations.note: Reflective/coreflective subcategory machinery captures special cases, but the general fixed-point equivalence induced by any adjunction is not formalized as a single result.

annotations.label: Adjunction gives rise to a monad

anchor.snippet: gives rise to an associated monad

annotations.note: `Adjunction.toMonad` builds the monad `G ⋙ F` from `L ⊣ R` (and `toComonad` dually).

anchors.snippet: gives rise to an associated monad

annotations.label: Every monad arises from an adjunction

anchor.snippet: Every monad arises from some adjunction

annotations.note: The Eilenberg–Moore adjunction `Monad.adj : T.free ⊣ T.forget` realizes any monad, with `Monad.adjToMonadIso : T.adj.toMonad ≅ T`.
